title: DengueSeq: A pan-serotype whole genome amplicon sequencing protocol for dengue virus
author: Chantal Vogels
materials: When program is complete, samples can remain in the thermocycler at 4°C or be stored at -20°C Briefly centrifuge tubes before use This is a safe stopping point. Amplicons can be stored long-term at -20°C.
When the program is complete, immediately remove the tubes from the thermocycler Briefly centrifuge tubes

steps.title: There are options available for use
steps.instruction: There are options available for use: Serotype-specific and Pan-serotype approach. Various library prep kits can be used with the DengueSeq primers, following the manufacturer's protocol. Below we describe detailed steps for the Illumina COVIDSeq Test Kit (RUO version), but other library prep kits for amplicon sequencing can be used as well. For example: Illumina COVIDSeq Test Kit Illumina Nextera XT DNA Library Preparation kit New England Biolabs NEBNext ARTIC SARS-CoV-2 Library Prep Kit (Illumina) Oxford Nanopore Technologies Rapid barcoding kit

steps.title: Within each serotype, organize odd and...
steps.instruction: Within each serotype, organize odd and even-numbered primers into two separate groups: Odd-numbered primers = Pool 1 Even-numbered primers = Pool 2 Recommended to organize numerically, i.e. 1_left, 1_right, 3-left, 3 right...

steps.title: If primers were ordered lyophilized, resuspend...
steps.instruction: If primers were ordered lyophilized, resuspend to 100 micromolar (µM), in nuclease-free water

steps.title: Add 90 µL, nuclease-free water to...
steps.instruction: Add 90 µL, nuclease-free water to two sets of 8-strip PCR tubes for each serotype: %s %s

steps.title: Label the tops of each tube...
steps.instruction: Label the tops of each tube with the corresponding primer that will be added For readability, recommended this format: D1 1L, D1 1R, D1 3L, D2 3R...

steps.title: Add 10 µL, 100 µM primer...
steps.instruction: Add 10 µL, 100 µM primer to each corresponding tube to reach 10 micromolar (µM), /primer Recommend to either mix tubes with a new pipette tip or gently vortex

steps.title: Working pool-by-pool, transfer 10 µL, 10µM primer into 1
steps.instruction: Working pool-by-pool, transfer 10 µL, 10µM primer into 1.5mL microcentrifuge tubes 8 tubes total, with 2 tubes (Pool 1 and Pool 2) per serotype Recommended to label each tube with serotype, concentration, pool #, and date of dilution This will be the working primer solution that will be added during the amplicon generation step

steps.title: Store all primers at -20ºC until use
steps.instruction: Store all primers at -20ºC until use

steps.title: Add 8
steps.instruction: Add 8.5 µL, EPH3 HT to new PCR tubes according to the number of samples Include one additional reaction for a cDNA NTC for each serotype-specific master mix (NTC), which will be included through the entirety of the protocol

steps.title: Add 8
steps.instruction: Add 8.5 µL, RNA to each tube Add 8.5 µL, nuclease-free water to the cDNA NTC Mix by pipetting up and down 10 times Briefly centrifuge tubes

steps.title: Load tubes into thermocycler and run...
steps.instruction: Load tubes into thermocycler and run the following program to generate first-strand cDNA: %s %s

steps.title: In a new 1
steps.instruction: In a new 1.5mL tube, prepare the following master mix according to the number of samples: %s %s All master mix volumes in this protocol are for one reaction and do not account for lost volume due to pipetting. Multiply volumes by reaction number accordingly.

steps.title: Add 8 µL, Master Mix to...
steps.instruction: Add 8 µL, Master Mix to each tube Mix by pipetting up and down 10 times Briefly centrifuge tubes

steps.title: Load tubes into thermocycler and run...
steps.instruction: Load tubes into thermocycler and run the following program to generate second-strand cDNA: %s %s

steps.title: Remove tubes from thermocycler and briefly...
steps.instruction: Remove tubes from thermocycler and briefly centrifuge This is a safe stopping point. cDNA can be stored long-term at -20°C.

steps.title: In two separate tubes, prepare the...
steps.instruction: In two separate tubes, prepare the following master mixes according to the number of samples: If processing more than one serotype in a run, prepare separate master mixes for each serotype, using the proper primer pools. Include one additional reaction for a PCR NTC for each serotype-specific master mix, which will be included through the entirety of the protocol. Pool 1 %s %s Pool 2 %s %s

steps.title: Distribute the master mixes
steps.instruction: Distribute the master mixes: Add 20 µL, Pool 1 Master Mix to a new set of PCR tubes, according to the number of samples Add 20 µL, Pool 2 Master Mix to a new set of PCR tubes, according to the number of samples Include one additional tube at the end of each set for the PCR NTC Be sure to match the serotype of each master mix to the previously-identified serotype of each sample

steps.title: Add 5 µL, cDNA to each...
steps.instruction: Add 5 µL, cDNA to each tube in both Pool 1 and Pool 2. Add 5 µL, nuclease-free water to the PCR NTC in both Pool 1 and Pool 2. Mix by pipetting up and down 10 times Briefly centrifuge tubes

steps.title: Load tubes into thermocycler and run the following program
steps.instruction: Load tubes into thermocycler and run the following program: %s %s

steps.title: Quantify samples on a Qubit to...
steps.instruction: Quantify samples on a Qubit to confirm proper amplification in both amplicon pools

steps.title: Prepare the following master mix
steps.instruction: Prepare the following master mix: %s %s

steps.title: Add 30 µL, Master Mix to...
steps.instruction: Add 30 µL, Master Mix to a new set of PCR tubes, according to the number of samples

steps.title: For each sample, combine amplicon pools
steps.instruction: For each sample, combine amplicon pools: Add 10 µL, Pool 1 to the Master Mix tubes Add 10 µL, Pool 2 to the Master Mix tubes Mix by pipetting up and down 10 times Briefly centrifuge tubes Remaining amplicon pools can be stored long-term at -20°C

steps.title: Load tubes into thermocycler and run the following program
steps.instruction: Load tubes into thermocycler and run the following program: %s %s

steps.title: Add 10 µL, ST2 to each...
steps.instruction: Add 10 µL, ST2 to each tube Mix by pipetting up and down Briefly centrifuge tubes

steps.title: Incubate at Room temperature, for 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate at Room temperature, for 5 minutes

steps.title: Place tubes on a magnetic stand...
steps.instruction: Place tubes on a magnetic stand and wait until the liquid is clear before continuing

steps.title: Remove and discard all supernatant Do...
steps.instruction: Remove and discard all supernatant Do not allow pipette tip to come into contact with the inner walls of the tube, as it can scrape away the magnetic beads

steps.title: Remove tubes from the magnetic stand...
steps.instruction: Remove tubes from the magnetic stand and add 100 µL, TWB HT to each tube (first wash) Mix by pipetting up and down 10 times Briefly centrifuge tubes Be careful not to introduce bubbles

steps.title: Repeat steps 5
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 5.9 - 5.11 (second wash) Do not remove supernatant after the second wash at this point

steps.title: Place the tubes on the magnetic stand
steps.instruction: Place the tubes on the magnetic stand

steps.title: Prepare the following Master Mix
steps.instruction: Prepare the following Master Mix: %s %s

steps.title: When the tubes with tagmented amplicons...
steps.instruction: When the tubes with tagmented amplicons (on the magnetic stand) are clear, remove and discard all supernatant Remove any remaining supernatant with a smaller-sized pipette.

steps.title: Remove tubes from the magnetic stand...
steps.instruction: Remove tubes from the magnetic stand and add 40 µL, Master Mix to each tube

steps.title: Briefly centrifuge the plate containing the...
steps.instruction: Briefly centrifuge the plate containing the dual-barcoded index adapters Clean the surface of the sealing foil with RNase Away Remove residual RNase away by wiping with 70% EtOH

steps.title: Add 10 µL, dual-coded index adapters...
steps.instruction: Add 10 µL, dual-coded index adapters to each well Mix by pipetting up and down 10 times Briefly centrifuge tubes

steps.title: Load tubes into thermocycler and run the following program
steps.instruction: Load tubes into thermocycler and run the following program: %s %s When program is complete, samples can remain in the thermocycler at 10°C or be stored at -20°C. This is a safe stopping point. Individual libraries can be stored long-term at -20°C.

steps.title: Place tubes on a magnetic stand...
steps.instruction: Place tubes on a magnetic stand and wait until the liquid is clear

steps.title: Pool libraries together in a new 1
steps.instruction: Pool libraries together in a new 1.5mL microcentrifuge tube by equal volume: %s %s For easier pooling of a large number of samples, it is recommended to multichannel the libraries into a new 8-strip of 0.2mL PCR tubes, then transfer the total volume to the microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Calculate the volume of ITB needed to reach 0.9x ITB
steps.instruction: Calculate the volume of ITB needed to reach 0.9x ITB : total pooled volume Example: 34 samples; 10µL of each sample pooled 34 * 10 = 340µL total pooled volume 340µL * 0.9 = 306µL ITB

steps.title: Add the calculated volume of ITB...
steps.instruction: Add the calculated volume of ITB to the pooled sample tube Vortex for 10 seconds to mix Briefly centrifuge

steps.title: Incubate at Room temperature, for 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate at Room temperature, for 5 minutes

steps.title: Place tube on a single-tube magnetic...
steps.instruction: Place tube on a single-tube magnetic stand and wait until the liquid is clear

steps.title: Remove and discard all supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove and discard all supernatant

steps.title: Add 1000 µL, 80% EtOH to...
steps.instruction: Add 1000 µL, 80% EtOH to the tube and incubate at Room temperature, for 30 seconds

steps.title: Repeat steps 7
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 7.7 - 7.8 and remove and discard supernatant after the second wash Remove any residual EtOH with a smaller-sized pipette

steps.title: Add RSB HT to the tube...
steps.instruction: Add RSB HT to the tube according to the number of samples pooled: For 1 - 24 samples add 30 µL, RSB HT For 25 - 48 samples add 40 µL, RSB HT For 49 - 96 samples add 50 µL, RSB HT

steps.title: Vortex tube to mix Briefly centrifuge tube
steps.instruction: Vortex tube to mix Briefly centrifuge tube

steps.title: Incubate at Room temperature, 2 minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate at Room temperature, 2 minutes

steps.title: Place on the magnetic stand and...
steps.instruction: Place on the magnetic stand and wait until the liquid is clear

steps.title: Transfer [total volume RSB] - 5µL to a new 1
steps.instruction: Transfer [total volume RSB] - 5µL to a new 1.5mL microcentrifuge tube

steps.title: Quantify library on a Qubit and...
steps.instruction: Quantify library on a Qubit and obtain fragment distribution using a Bioanalyzer/Tape Station

steps.title: Protocol validated on the Illumina NovaSeq (2x150)
steps.instruction: Protocol validated on the Illumina NovaSeq (2x150). For sequencing we recommend generating at least 1 million reads per sample for optimal sequencing coverage. Sequencing may be performed on Illumina and Oxford Nanopore Technologies sequencing platforms following standard protocols.

steps.title: Sequencing results may be analyzed utilizing...
steps.instruction: Sequencing results may be analyzed utilizing a standard amplicon sequencing bioinformatics pipeline, including those employed for SARS-CoV-2 sequencing (e.g. iVar). Bioinformatics pipeline: https://github.com/grubaughlab/DENV_pipeline/tree/v1.0 Reference Sequences: DENV1.fasta DENV2.fasta DENV2_sylvatic.fasta DENV3.fasta DENV4.fasta DENV4_sylvatic.fasta BED files: DENV1.bed DENV2.bed DENV2_sylvatic.bed DENV3.bed DENV4.bed DENV4_sylvatic.bed

steps.title: Organize odd and even-numbered primers into...
steps.instruction: Organize odd and even-numbered primers into two separate groups: Odd-numbered primers = Pool 1 Even-numbered primers = Pool 2 Recommended to organize numerically, i.e. 1_left, 1_right, 3-left, 3 right...

steps.title: If primers were ordered lyophilized, resuspend...
steps.instruction: If primers were ordered lyophilized, resuspend to 100 micromolar (µM), in nuclease-free water

steps.title: Add 80 µL, nuclease-free water to...
steps.instruction: Add 80 µL, nuclease-free water to two sets of 8-strip PCR tubes for each serotype: %s %s

steps.title: Label the tops of each tube...
steps.instruction: Label the tops of each tube with the corresponding primer that will be added For readability, recommended this format: 1L, 1R, 3L, 3R...

steps.title: Add 20 µL, 100 µM primer...
steps.instruction: Add 20 µL, 100 µM primer to each corresponding tube to reach 20 micromolar (µM), /primer Recommend to either mix tubes with a new pipette tip or gently vortex

steps.title: Combine 10 µL, 20µM primer of...
steps.instruction: Combine 10 µL, 20µM primer of each Pool 1 primer from all serotypes in a new 1.5mL microcentrifuge tube A total of 146 primers will be combined This tube will contain all Pool 1 primers for all serotypes Recommended to label each tube with concentration, pool #, and date of dilution This will be the working primer solution that will be added during the amplicon generation step Combine 10 µL, 20µM primer of each Pool 2 primer from all serotypes in a new 1.5mL microcentrifuge tube A total of 138 primers will be combined This tube will contain all Pool 2 primers for all serotypes Recommended to label each tube with concentration, pool #, and date of dilution This will be the working primer solution that will be added during the amplicon generation step

steps.instruction: Store all primers at -20ºC until use
